generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components //
instance SiemensSinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller acting as the intermediary system that processes control inputs (position, speed, acceleration) and generates outputs (target/actual values of position, velocity, and motor current) for the MABI MAX 100 robot's first axis."
]

instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor that converts electrical current into mechanical torque (τ_M) for the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot, operating within the drive train system."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the motor providing real-time angular position data (r_M) with reduced backlash and positional uncertainty for the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the cycloidal gearbox providing real-time angular position data (r_G) for the first axis, enabling backlash compensation and positional accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor input current (I_M) as an electrical input parameter for torque calculation and friction analysis in the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLFirstAxisAssetModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) and Internal Block Diagram (IBD) describing the structural composition of the first axis (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) and their rotational/current flows (torque τ, angular velocity ω, electrical current I_M)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance SimulinkFrictionlessBaseModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating frictionless behavior of the gearbox (Fig. 4a) as the baseline for friction analysis and compensation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationTool
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box analytical friction model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics for the cycloidal gearbox, used to subtract friction effects from the base model."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationTool
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data-driven black-box friction model implementing Stribeck curve characteristics to capture stiction and breakaway behavior in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationTool
]

instance MotorDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Engineering model converting measured current (I_M) into torque (τ_M) using manufacturer-provided parameters for the permanent magnet synchronous motor."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationTool
]

instance MeasuredMotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data of motor angular position (r_M) transmitted via MQTT from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller, including source metadata (MQTT channel, timestamp, units)."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance MeasuredGearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data of gearbox angular position (r_G) transmitted via MQTT, used for backlash compensation and positional accuracy validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data of motor input current (I_M) transmitted via MQTT, used for torque calculation and friction identification."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance SimulatedFrictionCompensatedOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated angular velocity outputs (δr_M, δr_G, δr_out) representing friction-compensated behavior derived from the friction models in Simulink."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulinkSimulationOutput
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DashboardVisualizationService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (Systems Modeling Language) tool used for structural and behavioral modeling of the robot's first axis, including component-connector diagrams and flow definitions."
    DTDFVocab:enables FirstAxisStructuralModelingService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink tool implementing executable simulation models for friction analysis (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) and system behavior validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionAccuracyImprovementService
]

instance MQTTDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport) protocol streaming real-time sensor data (position, current) from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller to the Digital Shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAcquisitionService
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM) providing the conceptual foundation for data traces, sources, processing components, and models in the friction analysis system."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance DashboardVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dashboard integration tool combining measured, simulated, and derived data for real-time monitoring of friction-induced deviations in the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DashboardVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FirstAxisStructuralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing SysML-based structural and behavioral modeling of the MABI MAX 100 robot's first axis for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides FirstAxisStructuralInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously identifying and analyzing static, kinetic, and viscous friction in the cycloidal gearbox using both white-box (Coulomb-Viscous) and black-box (Stribeck) models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyImprovementAction
]

instance DataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service responsible for real-time data acquisition from position encoders and current sensors via MQTT, enabling continuous friction monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceabilityInsight
]

instance MotionAccuracyImprovementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service identifying friction-induced errors (up to 30%) and providing data-driven corrections for control algorithms to improve motion accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterUpdateAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service detecting wear patterns or temperature-induced friction changes to schedule proactive maintenance based on real-time data analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides WearPatternInsight
]

instance DashboardVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating visualizations comparing measured vs. simulated behavior to highlight friction-induced anomalies in the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:provides DiagnosticReportInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FirstAxisStructuralInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural and behavioral description of the first axis components (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) derived from SysML modeling for friction analysis context."
]

instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantified characterization of static, kinetic, and viscous friction in the gearbox enabling targeted model refinement and control algorithm adjustments."
]

instance MotionAccuracyImprovementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Data-driven corrections applied to control algorithms to mitigate friction-induced inaccuracies (up to 30%) in the first axis motion."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataTraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Traceability of measured data (position, current) linked to simulation models via source metadata (MQTT channel, timestamp) for validation purposes."
]

instance ParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time adjustments to friction model parameters (e.g., Coulomb coefficients) for adaptive control without halting robot operation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance WearPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of wear patterns or temperature-induced friction changes in the gearbox enabling predictive maintenance scheduling."
]

instance DiagnosticReportInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualized comparison of measured (real) vs. simulated (frictionless) behavior highlighting friction-induced anomalies for diagnostic purposes."
]

// C12: Environment
instance FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor,
                 MotorPositionEncoder,
                 GearboxPositionEncoder,
                 MotorCurrentSensor,
                 SiemensSinumerik840DslController
]

instance FrictionAnalysisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SysMLFirstAxisAssetModel,
                 SimulinkFrictionlessBaseModel,
                 CoulombViscousFrictionModel,
                 StribeckFrictionModel,
                 MotorDynamicsModel
]

// C13: Components
instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox component in the first axis transmitting mechanical torque (τ_M) with friction characteristics requiring analysis for motion accuracy improvement."
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Gearwheel component in the first axis transmitting rotational motion between the motor and cycloidal gearbox, subject to backlash and friction effects."
]

instance FirstAxisDriveTrain : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Complete drive train assembly of the first axis including motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox with integrated position encoders for motion control."
]

// Added missing instance for SimulinkSimulationOutput
instance SimulinkSimulationOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output data from Simulink simulation models representing friction-compensated behavior for comparison with measured data."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital shadow operates on a **real-time, continuous time scale** with the following characteristics:
   - **Data acquisition rate**: Sensor data (e.g., position, current) is streamed at **high-frequency intervals** (not explicitly quantified in the document but implied by CNC control requirements for precision machining).
   - **Model update frequency**: Friction models are **continuously updated** during operation to account for dynamic changes (e.g., wear, temperature drift). The DS supports **low-latency processing** to maintain synchronization with the physical system.
   - **Time synchronization**: Data traces are annotated with **timestamps** to ensure temporal alignment between physical measurements and virtual simulations. The DS aligns with the **operational time scale** of the robot (e.g., machining cycles), enabling **real-time friction compensation** in control algorithms."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is organized as a **purpose-driven, modular system** centered around the **digital shadow of the first axis**:
   - **Centralized Architecture**: The DS is implemented as a **single, executable pipeline** linking physical measurements, engineering models, and simulation models. It does not describe a **distributed DT system** but focuses on **real-time friction analysis** for motion control.
   - **Component Orchestration**:
     - **Physical Layer**: The MABI MAX 100 robot (first axis) with sensors (encoders, current sensors) and the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller.
     - **Virtual Layer**: The DS integrates **SysML models**, **Simulink simulations**, and **MQTT-based data streams** into a unified processing pipeline.
     - **Processing Layer**: The **Simulation components** (SimModel_M, SimModel_GW, SimModel_GB) compute friction-compensated outputs using measured inputs and analytical models.
   - **Interoperability**: While not explicitly expanded, the DSRM and SysML models support **scalability** to include additional axes or systems (e.g., other robots) via **standardized data traces** and **Asset Administration Shells (AAS).**"
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital shadow covers the **operational phase** of the robot's life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** (real-world) behavior rather than design or manufacturing stages. Key aspects include:
   - **As-operated representation**: The DS models the **current state** of the first axis, including **wear, temperature effects, and dynamic friction** observed during machining operations.
   - **Continuous evolution**: The DS is designed for **online refinement** of friction models, enabling **adaptive maintenance** and **predictive diagnostics** during the robot's operational lifetime. It does not address **as-designed** (ideal) or **as-manufactured** (initial) states but rather **real-time deviations** from expected behavior.
   - **Purpose-driven lifecycle**: The DS is created to **improve motion accuracy** during operation, aligning with the **maintenance and optimization** phases of the robot's lifecycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity of the digital shadow are ensured through:
   - **Model Verification**:
     - **Analytical friction models** (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous) are validated against **manufacturer-provided parameters** and **real-world sensor data**.
     - **Simulink simulations** are cross-checked with **idealized behavior** (frictionless models) to quantify deviations.
   - **Data Validation**:
     - **Sensor calibration**: Encoders and current sensors are assumed to provide **accurate positional and electrical measurements** (no explicit calibration details provided).
     - **Traceability**: Each data point is linked to its **source (e.g., MQTT channel)**, **processing component (e.g., SimModel_GW)**, and **model parameters** (e.g., friction coefficients) for **auditability**.
   - **Uncertainty and Error Handling**:
     - **Backlash and elasticity** in the drive train are accounted for via **dual encoders** (motor and secondary encoder).
     - **Model uncertainty**: The DS supports **dynamic model exchange** to mitigate errors introduced by **wear or environmental changes** (e.g., temperature).
   - **Validation Mechanisms**:
     - **Deviation analysis**: Differences between **measured (r_M, r_G)** and **simulated (ˆr_M, ˆr_G)** data traces are analyzed to **identify friction-induced inaccuracies**.
     - **Parameter tuning**: Friction model parameters are **continuously adjusted** during operation to minimize errors."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital shadow (DS) involves:
   - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** is used for **real-time, low-latency data streaming** from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller to the DS.
   - **Data Format**: Sensor data (e.g., position, current) is transmitted as **time-series arrays** with **floating-point precision** (e.g., torque τ in Nm, angular velocity ω in °/s).
   - **Synchronization**: Data traces are annotated with **timestamps** to ensure **temporal alignment** between physical measurements and virtual simulations.
   - **Architecture**:
     - **One-way data flow**: The DS **receives** data from the PT but does not **send commands back** (consistent with digital shadow definition).
     - **Centralized processing**: The DS runs on a **local or cloud-based system** (not specified) to process incoming data and generate visualizations."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital shadow is deployed as a **software pipeline** with the following technical aspects:
   - **Execution Environment**: The DS runs on a **computational platform** (e.g., MATLAB Simulink for simulations, a dashboard for visualization). The exact hosting (local/cloud) is not specified but implied to be **co-located with the robot** for low-latency processing.
   - **Dependencies**:
     - **MATLAB Simulink**: Hosts the **simulation models** (friction, motor, gearbox).
     - **MQTT Broker**: Manages **real-time data streams** from the Siemens controller.
     - **Dashboard Tool**: Visualizes data traces (e.g., measured vs. simulated outputs) for **real-time monitoring**.
   - **Scalability**: The DSRM-based architecture supports **modular deployment** of additional DS instances for other axes or systems."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin system adheres to the following standards and frameworks:
   - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: Used for **structured modeling** of the robot's first axis (component-connector diagrams, flow definitions).
   - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: Provides a **conceptual framework** for data traces, sources, and processing components, ensuring **interoperability** with other digital shadow implementations.
   - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Implied as a potential enabler for **standardized data exchange** (though not explicitly instantiated in this use case).
   - **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)**: A **standardized protocol** for real-time data streaming from the Siemens controller to the DS."
]
